sections.content:
Interesting! The MOUSE click transformed the board significantly:
- The green frame is now a WHITE frame (144 pixels) - it's a 21x9 hollow rectangle
- Orange bars appeared on the left (18 pixels) and right (27 pixels) sides
- Red blocks are now on the LEFT side (18 pixels total, two 3x3 blocks)
- No green visible anymore!
- The white frame has hollow areas: rows 32-34 (two 3x3 holes), rows 41-43 (one 3x3 hole between red blocks)
Wait, looking more carefully:
- White shape: 144 pixels, forms a hollow rectangle from rows 29-49, cols 41-49
- The hollow areas are: rows 32-34 (cols 44-46), rows 38-40 (cols 44-46), rows 44-46 (cols 44-46)
- Actually no, looking at the ASCII:
```
Row 29-31: WWWWWWWWW  (solid top)
Row 32-34: WWW   WWW  (hollow middle)
Row 35-37: RRRWWW   WWW  (red blocks on left)
Row 38-40: WWW   WWW  (hollow middle)
Row 41-43: RRRWWW   WWW  (red blocks on left)
Row 44-46: WWW   WWW  (hollow middle)
Row 47-49: WWWWWWWWW  (solid bottom)
```
So the white shape is a hollow rectangle with 3 hollow sections (rows 32-34, 38-40, 44-46). The red blocks are on the left side, adjacent to the white shape.
The orange bars are on the left and right edges of the white shape.
This looks like a completely different puzzle state. The MOUSE action seems to have "activated" or "transformed" the puzzle.
Now I need to figure out what to do next. The goal might be to:
1. Move the red blocks into the hollow areas of the white shape
2. Or position something to complete the pattern
Let me try moving the red blocks. But wait - the red blocks don't seem to be movable independently. They're attached to the white shape or are separate objects.
Actually, looking at the segmentation, the red blocks (id=7, id=8) are separate nodes from the white shape (id=4). So they might be movable.
Let me try clicking on the red blocks to see if they move.
